name: Cape Cod Bay
description:
Cape Cod Bay is one of the most productive fishing grounds in the Northeast, enclosed by the hook of Cape Cod. The bay's sandy bottom, rocky ledges, and strong tidal currents create ideal habitat for both resident and migratory species.

Striped bass fishing is world-class from May through November, with the bay serving as a major staging area during the fall migration south. The annual tuna fishery draws anglers seeking bluefin exceeding 500 pounds. Numerous harbors and launch ramps around the bay provide access from both the Cape Cod and South Shore sides.

name: Buzzards Bay
description:
Buzzards Bay lies between Cape Cod and the South Coast, providing sheltered fishing waters with strong tidal currents. The Cape Cod Canal at the bay's northeast corner creates one of the most famous shore-fishing spots in New England.

Striped bass fishing is exceptional, with the canal producing fish over 50 pounds during the spring and fall migrations. Scup and tautog provide excellent bottom fishing around the bay's rocky structure. Fluke fishing on the sandy flats is productive during summer. Multiple harbors and launches along both shores provide easy access.

name: Quabbin Reservoir
description:
Quabbin Reservoir is Massachusetts' largest body of water and the primary drinking water supply for the Boston metropolitan area. Fishing access is carefully managed but the reservoir produces some of the finest lake trout and landlocked salmon in the state.

Lake trout exceeding 15 pounds are caught regularly in the deep, cold water. Landlocked salmon provide exciting topwater action during spring and fall. Fishing is restricted to specific access areas with special regulations, which helps maintain the exceptional water quality and fish populations. Three boat launch areas provide managed access.

name: Wachusett Reservoir
description:
Wachusett Reservoir is a large drinking water reservoir in central Massachusetts managed by the DCR. Shore fishing only is permitted, but the reservoir produces trophy lake trout and landlocked salmon.

Lake trout exceeding 10 pounds are caught from shore, primarily during spring when cold water brings fish within casting range. The shoreline access program provides designated fishing areas. The reservoir's clear, cold water and restricted access maintain an exceptionally healthy fishery. Several designated shore-fishing access points are available around the reservoir.

name: Lake Cochituate
description:
Lake Cochituate is a chain of three connected ponds in Cochituate State Park, located in the western suburbs of Boston. The lake provides accessible warm-water fishing within easy reach of the metro area.

Largemouth bass are the primary target, with fish exceeding 5 pounds caught regularly in the weed beds and around shoreline structure. Chain pickerel provide exciting action on light tackle. Spring trout stocking adds cold-water fishing opportunities. The state park boat launch provides convenient access with a car-top boat and small motor restriction.

name: Nantucket Sound
description:
Nantucket Sound lies between Cape Cod and the islands of Nantucket and Martha's Vineyard, creating warm, shallow fishing waters that attract migratory species. The sound's sandy shoals and tidal rips create excellent fish-holding structure.

Striped bass and bluefish provide surface action during the summer months. The fall run of false albacore and bonito has created a cult following among light-tackle and fly-fishing enthusiasts. Fluke fishing over the sandy bottom is productive all summer. Hyannis, Dennis, and Chatham harbors provide the primary launch access points.

name: Martha's Vineyard Waters
description:
The waters surrounding Martha's Vineyard offer some of the finest light-tackle saltwater fishing in the Northeast. The island's position creates rips, shoals, and current breaks that concentrate bait and game fish.

Lobsterville Beach and Wasque Point are legendary striper spots, producing fish over 50 pounds during the fall migration. Menemsha Basin provides access to excellent offshore fishing, and the fall false albacore blitz draws fly anglers from across the country. Several town launches and harbors provide boat access around the island.
